page: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is 32.0 GB, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes 236 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. The single-threaded gzip compression performance is in the top tier at 34.96 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 14.44 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth is average, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 335.66 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 44.69 GB/sec, though large block write bandwidth is weak at 22.41 GB/sec.

This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. It is suitable for caching layers, lightweight database operations, and memory-bound applications, as demonstrated by its average Redis SET performance of 7.86 million operations per second. However, its weak multi-core performance and poor medium-model LLM text generation speed make it less suitable for heavy parallel computing or large-scale AI inference. The inclusion of 236 GB of local storage provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring fast temporary storage without provisioning external disks.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, though multi-core performance and large-block memory write speeds are relatively weak. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. With its high memory density and local storage, the E4ds_v7 is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and lightweight database operations that benefit from local disk space but do not require heavy multi-threaded CPU scaling.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads and caching applications.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, running on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high memory-to-core density, and fast local SSD storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded compression and strong single-core performance
Suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and lightweight databases
